title: Archive: VTLA / Tactile Intuition (merged into DaiMeng VTAM)
description: Archived as a standalone Survey card after merging its tactile-intuition benchmark and representation scope into the DaiMeng VTAM/Tactile-WAM Research project.
summary: Standalone VTLA survey is no longer tracked as a separate live card. Its completed tactile intuition survey tasks now belong to the merged DaiMeng VTAM / Tactile-WAM Research project.

details.text: EgoVTLA idea: AprilTag + tactile patch glove, FK over egocentric video, contact as depth point plus force magnitude, and eye gaze for useful pixels. This should be scoped as perception/intention alignment, not only another tactile sensor build.

Tactile-Bolt / AnyInsert / InsertAnyShape idea: a tactile dataset and digital twin for bolts, caps, peg-in-hole, and insertion-style tasks. This is a strong survey candidate because it connects data-driven tactile simulation, tactile memory, and real contact-rich assembly.

Archived sub-idea 2026-06-05: 接近觉 / proximity / FingerEye is no longer live survey material. FingerEye already provides continuous pre-contact visual perception and post-contact tactile feedback in one compact fingertip sensor, so the remaining VTLA work should not spend a separate project slot on VL53L5CX-like proximity sensing unless we commit to a distinct lab hardware integration.

RobotCooking VTLA demo idea: tactile memory for avocado sandwich, opaque-bag search, peeling/cutting/plating. This should be treated as a long-horizon benchmark theme after the tactile representation is defined, not the first experiment.

details: Other open Notion items folded here: TouchNet / ViTaRAG, tactile as oracle for current/torque-loop control, emulating tactile from motor currents, fine-grained cylinder-rotation force control, articulated-object manipulation with tactile/force feedback, proprioception for object pose, and event-camera VLA as an additional modality.

details.text: Moved back from the mixed archive: tactile-glove wearable-by-human-and-robot ideas and full-palm tactile fusion should live here, not in Archive, because they are active ingredients for Wuji Glove, VTLA, and contact-rich manipulation benchmarks.

Notion migration addendum updated 2026-06-05: Tactile-Bolt / InsertAnyShape, EgoVTLA task-library, full-palm tactile-assisted human-to-robot dynamics transfer, UMI hand-vitac, DEXO/DEXOP-style hand exoskeleton capture, and Wuji/Franka tactile benchmark choices remain live survey material. Proximity sensing / FingerEye is now archived as a covered sub-idea.

details: Additional benchmark candidates from Notion: spilling/pouring water, cap/screw/bolt with tactile reconstruction, cylinder in-hand rotation, opaque-bag search, cutting/peeling/food prep, object handover/exchange, and DexH2R interaction tasks. These need a first-task selection before engineering.

details.text: Random Thought coverage 2026-06-05: the top-level research proposal is now explicitly mapped here. Its core target is human-robot perception alignment: move robot learning beyond vision-only perception by collecting human-worn glove/tactile/force data, then learning tactile/force dynamics models that predict visuo-tactile sensor outputs under actions. This remains live in VTLA because the unresolved part is not proximity sensing, but a Wuji/Franka/HOI benchmark and a reusable contact-state representation.

Placement update 2026-06-12: Tactile-WAM is a separate Survey card, not a Research card yet. VTLA should remain the broad tactile-representation and benchmark survey; the concrete Wuji proposal path lives in Tactile-WAM: external-camera retargeted robot video, tactile-from-video prediction, and WAM action output.

details: Placement update 2026-06-12: low-DoF tactile gripper/platform work moved here. The PSA three-finger tactile gripper, MANUS-to-two-finger retargeting, two-finger FinRay/GelSight/PPTAC-style platform, and 6-DoF tactile calibration platform are tactile/VTLA benchmark ingredients.

Merged into DaiMeng VTAM (2026-06-27): This survey card was folded into DaiMeng VTAM / Tactile-WAM: Predictive Contact Intelligence. Keep this archive only as historical context; all active work and completed VTLA tasks should appear under the merged Research card.
risks_decisions: The project should not become a shopping list of sensors; the first output needs a contact-state representation and a benchmarkable manipulation question.
Tactile data is expensive. Prioritize tasks where vision is genuinely occluded or ambiguous: insertion, cap/screw, opaque-bag search, cutting/peeling, and in-hand pose/contact estimation.
If tactile is only used as another observation channel, the novelty is weak. Stronger framing: tactile as oracle, memory, contact-field label, or failure/reward evidence.
Do not leave tactile glove / full-palm fusion notes in Archive if they feed Wuji/Franka, VTLA, or HOI data collection; keep this card for unresolved representation and benchmark choices.

Survey disposition 2026-05-31: keep, because ViTacFormer, ViTaSCOPE, and related sensor work prove the importance of visuo-tactile fusion but do not choose our first Wuji/Franka benchmark or data schema.
Archive decision 2026-06-05: proximity / FingerEye is now archived as a covered sub-idea because FingerEye completes the continuous pre-contact vision to post-contact tactile sensing route.
Next executable analysis: decide between tactile-bolt/insertion, opaque-bag search, and food/cutting as the first benchmark family, then list sensors, labels, and success metrics.
Archive only sub-ideas that have no path to Wuji/Franka/HOI data collection; keep full-palm tactile and glove-wearable ideas here.
Do not archive tactile glove or full-palm tactile notes just because a LEAP-hand subpage is archived; the live value is Wuji/Franka sensing, data schema, and the first tactile benchmark.

Random Thought coverage decision 2026-06-05: keep human-robot perception alignment, tactile/force dynamics, and glove/hand data-collection hardware inside VTLA; archive only sub-ideas that are directly completed by specific papers or have no path to Wuji/Franka/HOI data.
Tactile-WAM boundary 2026-06-11: keep it out of the generic VTLA survey. VTLA can supply tactile benchmarks and references; Tactile-WAM owns the executable demo chain and WAM action-output claim.

Hardware-platform boundary: Daimon/GelSight/PPTAC-like grippers and tactile calibration rigs should be evaluated as VTLA benchmark infrastructure; purchasing and lab availability details can reference Real-Robot Infra, but the representation/benchmark decision stays here.
asset_alt: Visuo-tactile manipulation diagram connecting vision, tactile patches, contact fields, and policy/world-model evidence
asset_caption: Survey diagram: tactile as contact evidence for policy, world-model prediction, and failure/reward signals.

references.title: Notion: Random Thought
references.notes: Original Notion source for human-robot perception alignment, glove/hand data-collection hardware, interaction-centric object/affordance/force representation, tactile/force dynamics model, hand graph/tree/chains, eigengrasp/PCA, active vision, RGB+XYZ, point-cloud resampling, and quantitative pouring/manipulation ideas.
